skill.name: testing-test-writing
skill.description: Guides AI agents to write focused unit and integration tests for core user flows, critical paths, and behavior-driven outcomes.
skill.summary: Write focused tests for important user flows and business behavior.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static path traversal findings are partly confirmed as a markdown instruction that points outside the scanned skill file. Static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives caused by ordinary words such as descriptive and standards, with no cryptographic operation present. No prompt injection, network access, command execution, or confirmed malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Instruction Reference Outside Skill File
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the agent to read ../../../agent-os/standards/testing/test-writing.md. This is not direct code execution, but it makes the skill depend on content outside the scanned file, so the full effective instructions were not verified in this audit.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative path traversal sequence is directly present in the skill instructions. Risk is medium because it references unscanned guidance rather than executing code or accessing secrets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static findings on SKILL.md lines 3, 15, and 24 appear to match substrings inside ordinary prose, not actual DES, MD5, RC4, or other cryptographic usage. No cryptographic operation or security-sensitive code is present in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language testing guidance and no cryptographic API calls or algorithms. The static pattern is a weak substring match.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal as Code Exploit
low_findings.description: The ../../../ sequence appears in a markdown link rather than executable code. It does not by itself read files, write files, or pass a path into a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is visibly a markdown reference, so classic path traversal exploitation is not present. Some residual risk remains because the referenced file is outside the scanned skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Parent Directory Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction references ../../../agent-os/standards/testing/test-writing.md. Marketplace review should verify referenced instruction files are bundled, scanned, or removed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory reference is directly visible in the only scanned file. It is risky for review completeness, not evidence of malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Write Focused Tests for Critical Paths
content.value_statement: Teams often write brittle tests that mirror implementation details instead of user behavior. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create lean tests for core workflows and critical logic.
actual_capabilities: Guides unit test and integration test creation for new features.
Prioritizes core user flows and business-critical paths.
Encourages behavior-focused assertions over implementation details.
Promotes clear, descriptive test case names.
Supports mocking of APIs, databases, and file systems.
Applies to common frameworks such as Jest, Mocha, RSpec, pytest, and JUnit.
limitations: The skill does not include executable test utilities or framework adapters.
The main instruction references an external standards file not included in the scanned skill.
It does not guarantee complete coverage for security, performance, or rare edge cases.
Generated tests still need project-specific review and execution.

use_cases.title: Add Tests During Feature Development
use_cases.description: Create focused tests that validate the main behavior of a new feature before release.

use_cases.title: Review Critical Workflow Coverage
use_cases.description: Identify the most important user journeys and add tests for business-critical outcomes.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Test Clarity
use_cases.description: Refactor brittle or unclear tests so they describe behavior and use cleaner names.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Unit Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one focused test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a focused unit test for this function. Test the expected behavior, use a clear test name, and avoid implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Cover a User Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature and propose tests for the primary user flow. Include the setup, action, expected result, and mocks needed.

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request involving APIs or databases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write tests for this code while mocking external dependencies. Keep the tests fast and verify observable behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Critical Path Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this change and create a focused test plan for critical paths. Separate must-have tests from lower-priority edge cases.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for tests for a checkout discount calculation.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes tests for eligible discounts, ineligible carts, and the final payable total. It avoids testing private helper methods.

output_examples.input: A team wants to add integration coverage for account creation.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the main signup path, required mocked services, expected database state, and user-visible success result.

output_examples.input: A reviewer finds a test suite that breaks after refactoring.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests renaming tests around behavior and replacing internal state assertions with externally visible outcomes.

best_practices: Start with tests for the primary user journey or business rule.
Name tests after the behavior users or systems can observe.
Mock slow or external dependencies while keeping important logic real.
anti_patterns: Do not test private implementation details when behavior can be observed.
Do not add broad edge-case suites before critical paths are covered.
Do not leave external service calls live in fast unit tests.

faq.question: What kinds of tests does this skill help write?
faq.answer: It helps with unit tests, integration tests, test files, fixtures, and mocks for important user flows.

faq.question: Does it choose a specific test framework?
faq.answer: No. It can guide work in common frameworks such as Jest, Mocha, RSpec, pytest, and JUnit.

faq.question: Will it generate exhaustive test coverage?
faq.answer: No. It favors minimal, high-value tests for critical paths and core behavior.

faq.question: Can it help with mocking?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports mocking APIs, databases, file systems, and other external dependencies.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has medium review risk because it references an external standards file outside the scanned skill file.

faq.question: What should publishers verify before release?
faq.answer: They should bundle or scan the referenced testing standards file, or replace the external link with complete instructions.
